findings.description: The document title is rendered as h1 but the section headings (ABOUT OUR AGENCY, ABOUT THE DIVISION, etc.) are all h2. This is acceptable hierarchy, but there is a nested <main> element which is a structural HTML issue, not a content fidelity issue.
findings.evidence: HTML contains <main><main>...</main></main> - double nested main elements. This is an HTML structure issue but does not affect content fidelity.

findings.description: The email address in the application instructions and the contact QUESTIONS section is not rendered as a clickable hyperlink in the HTML, whereas in the PDF it appears as a hyperlink (underlined, colored). The email text itself is accurate.
findings.evidence: PDF shows DEQInternships@ncdenr.gov as clickable links. HTML in application instructions shows plain text 'DEQInternships@ncdenr.gov' without <a> tag for the first instance, and the second instance at the bottom also lacks an <a> tag (just <u>DEQInternships@ncdenr.gov</u>).
